FR-01.title: Event Creation
requirements: Create events with title, description, date, time, and venue
Support multiple event types (conference, workshop, concert, webinar)
Upload event banner and promotional images
Set ticket types and pricing tiers

FR-03.title: Venue Management
requirements: Add venues with capacity, address, and floor plan
Seat map creation for assigned seating

FR-04.title: Speaker and Agenda Management
requirements: Add speakers with bio, photo, and topic
Create event agenda with time slots

FR-05.title: Attendee Check-in
requirements: QR code scanning at venue entrance

FR-06.title: Event Analytics
requirements: Ticket sales reports and revenue tracking

FR-07.title: Sponsorship Management
requirements: Sponsor profile pages with logo and description

FR-08.title: Email Campaign Management
requirements: Send invitation emails to mailing lists
Post-event thank you and survey emails

FR-09.title: Virtual Event Support
requirements: Live streaming integration for virtual events

FR-10.title: Refund and Cancellation
requirements: Attendee can request refund per policy
Event cancellation by organizer with full refund

FR-01.title: Plan Event Creation Workflow and Contracts
FR-01.description: Review the SRS requirement bullets for event creation and break them into executable API, UI, data, security, and reporting subtasks. Define the workflow states, user roles, field-level validations, request/response contracts, and failure cases before implementation starts. Use these requirement drivers as the baseline: Create events with title, description, date, time, and venue; Support multiple event types (conference, workshop, concert, webinar); Upload event banner and promotional images.
acceptance_criteria: The end-to-end workflow for event creation is documented from trigger to completion
Validation rules, role restrictions, and error scenarios for event creation are explicitly defined
Implementation subtasks for event creation can be picked up independently by engineering

FR-01.title: Implement Event Creation Backend Services
FR-01.description: Build the backend services for event creation on React, Python Django, PostgreSQL, Celery. Create route handlers or service endpoints, model the core business operations, enforce validation and authorization rules, persist workflow state changes, and add structured error handling and audit logging. Make sure the implementation covers these behaviors: Create events with title, description, date, time, and venue; Support multiple event types (conference, workshop, concert, webinar); Upload event banner and promotional images.
acceptance_criteria: Core event creation business rules are implemented for Event Management Platform
Validation, authorization, and error handling are covered for event creation
API/service behavior for event creation is testable and documented for integration

FR-01.title: Implement Event Creation Frontend Flow
FR-01.description: Build the frontend flow for event creation with the exact forms, tables, filters, wizards, status indicators, and action buttons required by the SRS. Connect the UI to the backend contract, handle optimistic updates or refresh flows where needed, and provide user-facing validation, permissions feedback, loading states, and recovery states.
acceptance_criteria: The event creation user flow is accessible and responsive across supported browsers
Forms, filters, tables, and feedback states for event creation behave correctly
The event creation UI is fully integrated with backend APIs and handles loading and error states

FR-01.title: Write Event Creation Automated and End-to-End Tests
FR-01.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for event creation. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for event creation are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for event creation are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical event creation scenarios

FR-02.title: Harden Ticket Booking Security and Access Rules
FR-02.description: Apply security controls to ticket booking. Implement role checks, field-level protections, data masking where appropriate, secure defaults, audit trails, and abuse-prevention rules. Verify that sensitive operations are guarded both in the API layer and in the UI visibility rules.
acceptance_criteria: Access control rules for ticket booking are enforced consistently across API and UI layers

FR-10.description: Write unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for refund and cancellation. Cover valid flows, invalid inputs, permission failures, state transitions, data integrity checks, and regression-prone edge cases pulled from the SRS. Add fixtures or mocks that make the feature verifiable in CI and local development.
acceptance_criteria: Happy path and failure cases for refund and cancellation are covered by automated tests
Permission checks and validation rules for refund and cancellation are verified
Regression coverage exists for the most critical refund and cancellation scenarios
